title: Add MAX_TOTAL_COMMITTED_USD cap — block new entries when total open exposure hits dollar ceiling
status: QUEUED — blocked until BANKROLL_AWARE_SIZING_001 closes (.env conflict)
prerequisite_task: BANKROLL_AWARE_SIZING_001 must be APPROVED before pickup (.env file conflict)
objective: Add a dollar-denominated cap on total open position exposure. Currently the bot only enforces a count cap (MAX_CONCURRENT_TRADES=3). With MAX_POSITION_SIZE_USD=$50, theoretical max exposure is $150 — but if sizing values are ever raised, there is no dollar ceiling. Add MAX_TOTAL_COMMITTED_USD as an env-gated hard block in check_entry_gates() in core/risk.py.
context: check_entry_gates() in core/risk.py already enforces MAX_CONCURRENT_TRADES (count cap) and MAX_TRADES_PER_MARKET. This task adds a third gate: if SUM(entry_px * qty WHERE open) + projected_new_size > MAX_TOTAL_COMMITTED_USD, block entry. The committed sum must be read from the live DB, not from in-memory state, so it is always accurate. The new size to add can be estimated as MAX_POSITION_SIZE_USD (worst case) or passed in. Use MAX_POSITION_SIZE_USD as the conservative estimate for gate evaluation.
do_not_touch: Any file not listed in allowed_files above

instructions: Add MAX_TOTAL_COMMITTED_USD=150 — this is the default dollar ceiling for total open exposure.
This matches the theoretical maximum (3 trades × $50 cap). It can be lowered later to tighten risk.
If BANKROLL_AWARE_SIZING_001 has already been applied, add this alongside the existing new entries — do not overwrite them.
Read the full file before editing. Locate check_entry_gates() and understand all existing gates.
Add a new env read near the top of the file alongside existing env reads: MAX_TOTAL_COMMITTED_USD = float(os.getenv('MAX_TOTAL_COMMITTED_USD', '150'))
Inside check_entry_gates(), after the existing count and per-market gates, add the new dollar exposure gate.

The gate logic: query the DB for current committed capital = SELECT COALESCE(SUM(entry_px * qty), 0) FROM trades WHERE status='open'. Then check: if current_committed + MAX_POSITION_SIZE_USD > MAX_TOTAL_COMMITTED_USD: return False, ['exposure_cap_exceeded:committed=X cap=Y']
Use the same DB connection pattern already present in risk.py or pass the DB path from the caller context. Do not introduce a new DB path pattern.
If MAX_TOTAL_COMMITTED_USD == 0, treat as disabled (skip gate). This preserves the env-gated disable pattern used elsewhere.
The rejection reason string must follow the existing format used by other gates in check_entry_gates() so logging and dashboard display are consistent.
Do not change any existing gate logic, thresholds, or function signatures.

acceptance_criteria: core/risk.py reads MAX_TOTAL_COMMITTED_USD from env with default 150
check_entry_gates() blocks entry when current_committed + MAX_POSITION_SIZE_USD > MAX_TOTAL_COMMITTED_USD
Gate returns False with descriptive reason string matching existing format
All existing gates in check_entry_gates() are untouched
verification: Read the updated check_entry_gates() and confirm gate is in correct position (after count/market gates)
Confirm rejection reason string format matches existing gate rejections in the same function
Grep risk.py for MAX_TOTAL_COMMITTED_USD — expect exactly one env read and one usage
Confirm MAX_TOTAL_COMMITTED_USD=0 path is handled (gate skipped)
rollback: Remove MAX_TOTAL_COMMITTED_USD gate block from check_entry_gates() in risk.py. Remove MAX_TOTAL_COMMITTED_USD from .env. Restart bot.
restart_note: Restart required after approval for gate to take effect.
deliverables: Exact lines added to risk.py with before/after context
